assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with MIXED — both additive and reductive signals are present across the test file changes
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | Modified test file has both additive and reductive signals; new test file is additive |'. The report explicitly classifies the test changes as MIXED and explains both additive and reductive signals.

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers as a reductive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 41: '- +1 test function (`test_recommend_purls_dedup`), -1 test function (`test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers`)'. Also line 50: 'Reductive findings: - `tests/api/purl_recommend.rs`: `test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers` removed (tested qualifier inclusion behavior that no longer exists in the API).'

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the relaxed assertion in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers
assertion_results.evidence: The structural summary at line 42 mentions '+2 assertions in `test_recommend_purls_basic` (added `!contains("?")` checks)' and notes the assertion was updated, but it does not explicitly identify the change in the PURL assertion as a relaxed/reductive signal. The structural summary treats the test_recommend_purls_basic changes as additive (strengthened assertions). The reductive findings section (line 50) only mentions the removed test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers. The fact that the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL (with qualifiers) to a versioned PURL (without qualifiers) is noted in criterion-1.md and the Test Requirements Detail, but it is not identified as a reductive signal or relaxed assertion in the structural summary or reductive findings.

assertion_results.text: The new test file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs is recognized as additive (3 new test functions)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 52-54: '**New file: `tests/api/purl_simplify.rs`** Classification: ADDITIVE (new file with 3 new test functions and 10 assertions)'

assertion_results.text: The new test function test_recommend_purls_dedup in the modified file is recognized as an additive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 41: '+1 test function (`test_recommend_purls_dedup`)' in the structural summary. Line 56: 'the modified test file contains both additive signals (new test function, strengthened assertions) and reductive signals'. The new test_recommend_purls_dedup is explicitly counted as an additive signal.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only analysis/report files: report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. All files are verification reports and criterion assessments. No source code files (e.g., .rs, .toml, .py) were created or modified in the outputs. The report itself is a read-only verification document analyzing existing PR changes.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md ends with 'Overall: PASS' and observations/findings, but does not contain any merge commands, auto-merge instructions, or merge actions. The report is structured as a verification document for human review with sections like 'Observations' noting potential issues (dedup_by limitation, total count inflation). The footer reads '*This comment was AI-generated by [sdlc-workflow/verify-pr]...*' indicating it's a comment for review, not an automated merge action.
